title: Prefix-Tuning: Optimizing Continuous Prompts for Generation
hook: What if you could adapt huge language models by tuning just a tiny sliver?
promise: You will understand how prefix-tuning lets you steer language models with minimal extra parameters.
prerequisite_note: Bring an intuition for how language models use prompts to guide text generation.

glossary.plain: A method that adapts language models by learning small trainable vectors prepended to the input.
Updating all parameters of a pretrained model for a new task.
Supplying a model with a task description or example to guide its output.
Learned vectors that act like extra input tokens but are not actual words.
Model weights that do not change during adaptation or training.
A setting where only a small amount of training data is available.

cards.title: Adapt Big Models with Small Tweaks
body: Learn how prefix-tuning adapts massive language models using only a sliver of new parameters.

cards.title: How can we teach a giant model something new?
body: Imagine you have a huge language model, but you want it to master a new task without retraining everything.
Fine-tuning the full model is powerful, but storing a separate copy for each task is wasteful and slow. Is there a lighter way?

cards.title: From prompts to prefixes to new skills
body: Traditional prompting guides a model with text, while fine-tuning rewrites all its knowledge.
Prefix-tuning finds a middle path: it learns a compact, task-specific prefix vector that steers the model, with almost everything else left untouched.
cards.caption: Prefix-tuning attaches a small prompt-like vector, letting the model adapt without changing its core.

cards.title: Fine-tuning vs. Prefix-tuning: What changes?
body: Imagine you want GPT-2 to summarize news articles for different domains.
cards.offNote: You update and store all model weights for each new task, using lots of space.
cards.onNote: You only learn and store a tiny prefix vector, keeping the main model frozen.

cards.title: Tiny prefixes, big effects
body: Instead of retraining the entire model, prefix-tuning learns a short, continuous vector that acts like a prompt.
This vector is prepended to every input, letting the model focus on the new task while keeping its original abilities.

cards.title: How much do you need to tune?
body: Adjust how much of the model you change for each new task.
cards.low: Only use prompts, no learning, so limited control over the model.
cards.mid: Prefix-tuning: learn a small prefix vector, balancing efficiency and control.
cards.high: Fine-tune all parameters, giving full control but at high storage and compute cost.

cards.title: What goes into prefix-tuning?
body: Pick the ingredients that make prefix-tuning work for adapting language models.

choices.label: Frozen pretrained model
choices.note: Keeps the main model unchanged during adaptation.

choices.label: Learned prefix vectors
choices.note: Small trainable vectors that guide the model.

choices.label: Attention mechanism
choices.note: Lets the model attend to both prefix and input tokens.

choices.label: Task-specific prefix
choices.note: Each new task gets its own unique prefix.

choices.label: Virtual tokens
choices.note: Prefix vectors act as extra, non-word tokens.

choices.label: Minimal storage overhead
choices.note: Only the prefix needs to be stored per task.

cards.title: Tiny prefixes, strong results
body: Prefix-tuning matched or beat full fine-tuning on table-to-text and summarization tasks, even with just 0.1 percent as many parameters.
It excelled when training data was scarce and also generalized better to new, unseen topics.
cards.caption: Prefix-tuning achieves strong results with minimal parameter updates, especially when data is limited.

cards.title: A new path for efficient adaptation
body: Prefix-tuning showed that you can steer giant models with just a few trainable vectors, saving space and effort.
This approach opened the door to adapting one model to many tasks without the burden of storing many full copies.

recap.label: Learn
recap.note: Train a small, task-specific prefix vector.

recap.label: Prepend
recap.note: Attach the prefix to every input.

recap.label: Guide
recap.note: Let the prefix steer generation for new tasks.

cards.title: Prefix-Tuning: Optimizing Continuous Prompts for Generation
body: Read the original paper for full details and experimental results.
